title: Thought
content:
I am not poor, but I am proud,
    Of one inalienable right,
    Above the envy of the crowd,--
    Thought's holy light.

    Better it is than gems or gold,
    And oh! it cannot die,
    But thought will glow when the sun grows cold,
    And mix with Deity.

    BOSTON, 1823.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Ralph Waldo Emerson delivers a powerful performance in "Thought"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
